title: Asteroids Vector Shooter
brief: Thrust-and-rotate ship blasting splitting asteroids with wraparound physics.
prompt: Build a complete Asteroids arcade game as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources, canvas/WebAudio only, runs fully offline). Render a vector-style ship on a black canvas. Controls: Left/Right arrows rotate, Up arrow applies thrust with inertia (velocity carries, gentle friction), Spacebar fires bullets. All objects (ship, bullets, asteroids) wrap around screen edges. Large asteroids drift with random velocity/rotation; shooting a large one splits it into two medium, medium into two small, small ones vanish. Bullets have limited lifetime/range. Ship-asteroid collision destroys the ship: show a brief explosion (particle burst), decrement lives (start with 3), and respawn at center with brief invulnerability. Track and display score (bigger points for smaller asteroids) and lives in a HUD. When all asteroids are cleared, spawn a new larger wave. Game over screen when lives reach 0 with a restart option. Add synthesized WebAudio for thrust, firing, and explosions. Use delta-time so motion is framerate-independent.

title: Space Invaders
brief: Marching alien grid, destructible bunkers, descending shots, and waves.
prompt: Build a Space Invaders arcade game as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources, canvas + synthesized WebAudio only, fully offline). Render a grid of aliens (e.g. 5 rows x 11) that move horizontally in lockstep, drop down a row and reverse direction upon hitting an edge, and speed up as fewer aliens remain (the classic accelerating march with a stepping sound). The player ship at the bottom moves Left/Right and fires upward with Spacebar (only one or two player bullets on screen at a time). Aliens randomly fire bombs downward. Include 3-4 destructible bunkers above the player that erode chunk-by-chunk when hit by either side's fire. An occasional UFO flies across the top for bonus points. Hitting an alien removes it and scores points (different rows worth different values). Player loses a life when hit by a bomb; start with 3 lives shown in a HUD alongside score. Lose if aliens reach the bottom or all lives are gone. Clearing the grid spawns a faster new wave that starts lower. Provide a game-over/restart screen.

title: Frogger Crossing
brief: Grid-hop a frog across traffic lanes and a log-filled river to home slots.
prompt: Build a Frogger-style crossing game as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources, canvas only, synthesized WebAudio for hops/deaths, fully offline). Lay out a grid: a starting safe zone at the bottom, several road lanes with cars/trucks moving at different speeds and directions, a middle safe median, then a river with logs and turtles drifting horizontally, and a top row of 5 home slots. Arrow keys move the frog one grid cell per press (up/down/left/right) with a small hop animation. On the road, touching any vehicle kills the frog. On the river, the frog DROWNS unless standing on a log/turtle, and rides along with whatever it stands on (carried off-screen = death). Reaching an empty home slot scores points and resets the frog to start; filling all 5 slots completes the level and starts a faster one. Start with 3 lives and an on-screen timer that, if it expires, costs a life. Show score, lives, and level in a HUD. Game over when lives run out, with a restart option.

title: Missile Command
brief: Aim explosions to intercept raining missiles before they destroy your cities.
prompt: Build a Missile Command arcade game as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources, canvas + synthesized WebAudio only, fully offline). Display 6 cities along the bottom plus 3 missile batteries each holding a limited ammo count. Enemy missiles streak down from random points at the top toward cities/batteries, drawing visible trails. The player aims with the mouse and clicks to launch a counter-missile from the nearest battery to the cursor; the counter-missile travels to that point then detonates into an expanding-then-contracting blast circle. Any enemy missile touching an active blast is destroyed (chain explosions feel satisfying). Enemy missiles that reach the ground destroy whatever they hit. A wave ends when all incoming missiles are gone; show a wave-complete bonus for remaining cities and unused ammo, then start a harder wave (more/faster missiles, some splitting into MIRVs). Batteries reload ammo between waves. HUD shows score, wave number, and ammo per battery. Game over when all cities are destroyed, with a restart option.

title: 2048 Sliding Tiles
brief: 4x4 2048 with arrow-key merges, sliding animations, score, win/lose states
prompt: Build a 2048 puzzle game as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources). Render a 4x4 grid of numbered tiles. Arrow keys (and WASD) slide all tiles in that direction; tiles with equal values that collide merge into their sum, and each tile may merge at most once per move. After every valid move spawn one new tile (value 2 with ~90% chance, else 4) in a random empty cell; do nothing if the move changes nothing. Use distinct background colors per tile value (2,4,8,...,2048+) with readable text. Animate tile slides and merges smoothly. Show the current score (incremented by merge values) and a best score for the session. Detect and announce a win when a 2048 tile appears (allow continuing) and game over when no moves remain. Include a New Game button that resets the board. Make it polished, centered, and visually pleasing.

title: Minesweeper
brief: Classic minesweeper: reveal, flood-fill, flags, mine counter, timer, win/lose
prompt: Build a Minesweeper game as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources). Generate a grid (default 16x16 with 40 mines) where mines are placed randomly but never on the first clicked cell. Left-click reveals a cell; if it has zero adjacent mines, flood-fill to reveal all connected zero cells and their numbered borders. Right-click toggles a flag (and prevent flagged cells from being revealed). Show adjacent-mine counts 1-8 in the conventional distinct colors. Include a mine counter (mines minus flags placed), an elapsed-time counter that starts on first reveal, and a reset/smiley button. Revealing a mine ends the game and shows all mines; flagging/revealing all safe cells correctly triggers a win. Provide difficulty buttons (Beginner 9x9/10, Intermediate 16x16/40, Expert 30x16/99). Make the styling clean and the cells crisp.

title: Sokoban Levels
brief: Grid box-pusher with walls, goals, 4+ levels, move/push counter, undo, reset
prompt: Build a Sokoban puzzle game as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources). Represent levels on a tile grid with walls, floor, boxes, goal targets, and the player. Arrow keys (and WASD) move the player one tile; the player can push a single box into an empty floor or goal tile but cannot push two boxes at once or push a box into a wall. Boxes resting on goals are visually highlighted. A level is solved when every goal tile holds a box, triggering a win message and advance to the next level. Include at least 4 distinct hand-designed solvable levels of increasing difficulty, a move counter and push counter, an Undo button (multi-step history), a Reset Level button, and Previous/Next level controls. Draw clear distinct tiles for each element and center the board. Style it cleanly.

title: Lights Out
brief: 5x5 toggle puzzle: click flips cross neighbors, solver hint, random solvable boards
prompt: Build a Lights Out puzzle as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources). Render a 5x5 grid of light cells that can be on or off. Clicking a cell toggles that cell and its orthogonal neighbors (up/down/left/right) between on and off. The goal is to turn all lights off. Generate new boards by starting from all-off and applying a random set of clicks so every puzzle is guaranteed solvable. Track and display the number of moves taken. Detect the solved state (all lights off) and show a congratulations message. Include a New Puzzle button, a Reset button (return to the current puzzle's start), and a Hint button that highlights a cell belonging to a valid solution (compute the solution via GF(2) linear algebra / the chase-the-lights method). Animate the on/off transitions and style the board attractively with a clear on vs off appearance.

title: Connect Four vs AI
brief: 7x6 Connect Four vs minimax AI, animated drops, win highlight, difficulty
prompt: Build a Connect Four game (player vs AI) as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources). Render a 7-column by 6-row board. The human (red) clicks a column to drop a disc into the lowest empty slot, with a smooth falling drop animation; hovering a column previews the drop. The AI (yellow) then responds. Implement the AI with minimax plus alpha-beta pruning and a heuristic board evaluation (favoring center control and counting open 2-/3-in-a-row threats), with a depth that makes it genuinely challenging but fast. Detect wins for four-in-a-row horizontally, vertically, or diagonally and highlight the winning four discs; detect draws when the board fills. Show whose turn it is and a result banner. Include a difficulty selector (Easy/Medium/Hard mapping to search depth) and a New Game button. Style the board with the classic blue frame and circular slots, centered and polished.

title: Othello vs AI
brief: Reversi/Othello on 8x8 with legal-move hints and a greedy/corner-aware AI
prompt: Build a playable Othello (Reversi) game as a single self-contained HTML file. Render an 8x8 green board with the standard 4-disc starting position. The human plays Black, the AI plays White. On the human's turn, highlight all legal moves with small dots; clicking a legal cell places a disc and flips all bracketed opponent discs in the 8 directions with a brief flip animation. Enforce full Othello rules: a move is legal only if it flips at least one disc; if a player has no legal move their turn is skipped (show a 'passed' notice); the game ends when neither player can move. The AI must use a heuristic stronger than random — weight corners highly, avoid squares adjacent to empty corners, and otherwise maximize discs flipped (a 1-2 ply search is fine). Show a live score (disc count per color), whose turn it is, and a clear end-of-game result with a 'New Game' button. Include a subtle valid-move count or hint toggle. No external assets.

title: Yahtzee Dice
brief: 5-dice Yahtzee: hold/reroll up to 3, full 13-category scorecard, totals
prompt: Build a single-player Yahtzee-style dice game as a single self-contained HTML file. Draw 5 dice with proper pip faces (1-6) using CSS or SVG. Each turn the player gets up to 3 rolls: clicking a die toggles a 'hold', and a Roll button rerolls only un-held dice (disable it after the 3rd roll). Provide a full 13-category scorecard: Ones through Sixes (upper section, with the 35-point bonus if the upper subtotal reaches 63), plus Three of a Kind, Four of a Kind, Full House (25), Small Straight (30), Large Straight (40), Yahtzee (50), and Chance. After rolling, show the score each open category WOULD earn if chosen (preview); clicking a category commits that score, ends the turn, and resets to 3 rolls. Correctly compute every category from the dice. Track upper subtotal, bonus, and grand total live, and announce the final total after all 13 categories are filled, with a New Game button. Animate the dice on roll. No external assets.

title: Mastermind
brief: Guess the 4-peg secret code; black/white peg feedback over 10 rows
prompt: Build the code-breaking game Mastermind as a single self-contained HTML file. The computer picks a secret code of 4 pegs from 6 colors (duplicates allowed). Provide a 10-row guess board: the player selects a color for each of the 4 positions in the current row (clicking a slot cycles colors, or pick from a palette) and submits the guess. For each submitted guess, render feedback peg(s): one BLACK peg for each peg that is the correct color in the correct position, and one WHITE peg for each correct color in the wrong position — using the standard exact algorithm that never double-counts a peg. Lock submitted rows. The player wins on 4 black pegs (reveal a win message); if all 10 rows are used without cracking it, reveal the secret code as a loss. Show a New Game button that generates a fresh secret. Include a small color legend and prevent submitting an incomplete row. No external assets.

title: Checkers vs AI
brief: 8x8 draughts: diagonal moves, forced jumps, multi-jumps, kings, simple AI
prompt: Build a game of Checkers (draughts) as a single self-contained HTML file on an 8x8 board with the standard 12-vs-12 starting layout on dark squares. The human plays the bottom color, a simple AI plays the top. Implement standard rules: men move diagonally forward one square to an empty dark square; capture by jumping a diagonally-adjacent opponent piece into an empty square beyond it; multi-jumps continue in one turn; reaching the far row promotes a man to a KING (drawn distinctly) which moves and jumps both directions. Enforce mandatory capture: if any jump is available the player must take a jump. Highlight the selected piece and its legal destinations; animate or clearly show moves and remove captured pieces. The AI should prefer captures (ideally multi-captures) and otherwise make a reasonable move. Detect game over when a side has no pieces or no legal moves, announce the winner, and offer a New Game button. No external assets.

title: Blackjack
brief: Hit/stand/double vs dealer-17 AI, animated card deal, chip betting
prompt: Build a single-player Blackjack game versus a dealer as a single self-contained HTML file. Use a shuffled 52-card deck drawn as styled card faces (rank + suit, red/black) with CSS/SVG — no images. The player starts with a chip bankroll (e.g. 500); they place a bet, then both player and dealer are dealt two cards with the dealer's second card face-down. Provide Hit, Stand, and Double Down actions. Compute hand totals correctly with Aces counting as 11 or 1 to avoid busting, and detect a natural Blackjack (pays 3:2). On Stand, flip the hole card and have the dealer draw until reaching 17 or more (dealer stands on all 17). Resolve the round: bust, win, lose, or push, and update the bankroll accordingly. Animate cards being dealt. Show both totals, the current bet, and the bankroll; offer a Deal/Next Hand button to start a new round, and handle running out of chips gracefully. No external assets.

title: Cave Flyer
brief: Hold-to-thrust helicopter dodging procedurally scrolling cave walls.
prompt: Build a side-scrolling cave-flyer game as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources, fully offline). Render on an HTML canvas. The player controls a small craft on the left side of a horizontally scrolling cave. Holding the mouse button or Spacebar applies upward thrust against constant gravity, so the craft accelerates up while held and falls when released, giving smooth momentum-based flight. Procedurally generate the cave as a top and bottom terrain band that gets narrower and more jagged over time as scroll speed gradually increases, so difficulty ramps up. Add occasional stalactite/pillar obstacles protruding into the gap. Colliding with any wall or obstacle ends the run with a game-over overlay showing the final distance score and a restart prompt (click or key). Display a live distance/score counter that increases as you travel. Show a brief 'hold to thrust' instruction on the start screen. Include a particle exhaust trail behind the craft for visual feedback. Use a clean neon-on-dark visual style, keep a fixed timestep or delta-time loop so motion is frame-rate independent, and ensure collisions feel fair (hitbox slightly smaller than the sprite).

title: Mini Golf
brief: Drag-to-aim mini-golf with walls, friction, and multiple holes.
prompt: Build a 2D mini-golf game as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources, fully offline). Render a top-down course on an HTML canvas. The ball rests on a green; the player aims by pressing on the ball and dragging backward like a slingshot, with a visible aim line and a power indicator that scales with drag distance (capped at a max power). Releasing launches the ball; it rolls with realistic friction/deceleration and bounces off the course walls and rectangular obstacles with proper reflection. The hole is a circular cup that captures the ball when it arrives slowly enough; if it is rolling too fast it lips out. Include at least 3 distinct hand-designed holes with walls and obstacles, advancing automatically when the cup is made. Track and display the stroke count per hole and a running total (par optional). Show a 'Hole N' label and a final scorecard after the last hole with a replay button. Disallow re-launching while the ball is still moving. Use a polished, readable visual style and frame-rate-independent physics.

title: Stack Tower
brief: Time-the-drop block stacker; overhang gets sliced off each level.
prompt: Build a stack-the-blocks tower game as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources, fully offline). Render on an HTML canvas. A horizontal block slides back and forth across the top of the screen; the player presses (click or Spacebar) to drop it onto the stack below. The portion of the dropped block that overhangs the block beneath it is sliced off and falls away (animate the falling sliver), and the new block's width becomes only the overlapping region, so precision matters. Each successful placement increases the score and shifts the camera/view upward so the growing tower stays in frame. The sliding speed increases gradually as the tower grows. If a drop misses entirely (zero overlap) the game ends with a game-over overlay showing the final height/score and a restart option. Reward near-perfect drops (overlap within a small tolerance) with a 'perfect' bonus and a brief visual flash, and on a perfect drop do NOT shrink the block. Use a pleasant shifting color palette per level (compute colors procedurally), smooth easing on the camera, and frame-rate-independent motion.

title: Pinball
brief: 2D pinball: two flippers, bumpers, plunger launch, ball physics.
prompt: Build a 2D pinball game as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources, fully offline). Render a vertical pinball table on an HTML canvas. Implement gravity-driven ball physics with circle-vs-line and circle-vs-circle collision response and energy-preserving bounces. Provide two flippers at the bottom controlled by the Left and Right arrow keys (or A/L): pressing rotates a flipper up quickly and it springs back when released, imparting velocity to the ball on contact. Include a plunger/launch mechanic to put the ball into play (hold to charge power, release to fire up the launch lane). Add at least three circular bumpers that kick the ball away and award points, plus angled walls/slingshots and table boundaries. If the ball drains between the flippers, deduct a ball from a count of 3; show balls remaining and a live score. After all balls drain, show a game-over overlay with final score and a restart. Tune physics so the ball doesn't tunnel through thin walls (use sub-stepping or continuous checks) and keep motion frame-rate-independent. Use a clear, arcade-style visual design with score display.

title: Sky Hopper
brief: Auto-bounce vertical platformer; tilt left/right across endless platforms.
prompt: Build a Doodle-Jump-style vertical platformer as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources, fully offline). Render on an HTML canvas in portrait orientation. The character automatically bounces upward whenever it lands on a platform (no jump button); the player moves left and right with the Arrow keys (or A/D), and the character wraps around the screen edges horizontally. As the character rises past the midline, the view scrolls down so it stays centered, and new platforms are procedurally generated above while old ones below are culled. Score equals the maximum height climbed. Include platform variety: solid platforms, moving platforms that slide horizontally, and fragile/breakable platforms that crumble after one bounce. Add a spring or trampoline power-up on some platforms that launches the character much higher. If the character falls below the bottom of the visible area, the game ends with a game-over overlay showing the final score and a restart prompt. Increase platform spacing/difficulty gradually with height. Use procedurally drawn graphics, a squash-and-stretch bounce animation for juice, and frame-rate-independent physics.

title: 5-Letter Word Guess
brief: Wordle-style daily word game with 6 guesses and colored letter feedback
prompt: Build a 5-letter word guessing game as a single self-contained HTML file. Embed a built-in list of at least 200 valid 5-letter answer words plus a larger acceptance list. Pick a secret word at random on load. Give the player 6 guesses entered via an on-screen QWERTY keyboard and physical keyboard. After each guess, color each tile: green for correct letter+position, yellow for present-but-wrong-position, gray for absent — handling duplicate letters correctly (a yellow only shows if an unmatched copy exists). Reflect the same coloring on the on-screen keyboard keys. Reject guesses not in the word list with a shake animation and a brief message. Flip-reveal tiles with a CSS animation row by row. Show a win banner with the guess count or reveal the answer on loss, plus a 'New Game' button to restart with a fresh random word. Track and display win/loss stats for the session.

title: Typing Speed Test
brief: Timed typing test computing live WPM, accuracy, and per-char highlighting
prompt: Build a typing speed test as a single self-contained HTML file. Display a paragraph of sample text (rotate among at least 8 built-in passages) with each character rendered as a span. As the user types into a hidden or styled input, highlight the current character with a caret, color correctly-typed characters green and mistakes red, and do not let the user skip ahead past errors visually. Start a timer on the first keystroke. Show live updating WPM (computed as (correct characters / 5) / minutes), accuracy percentage, elapsed time, and error count. Offer selectable test durations (15s, 30s, 60s) or run until the passage ends. When the test ends, show a results card with final WPM, accuracy, raw WPM, and characters typed, plus a 'Restart' button that loads a new random passage. Include clean, focused styling and a visible progress indicator.

title: Mental Math Blitz
brief: 60-second arithmetic sprint with difficulty scaling, streaks, and scoring
prompt: Build a mental math blitz game as a single self-contained HTML file. Present randomly generated arithmetic problems (addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division with whole-number answers) one at a time. The player types the answer into an input; pressing Enter or auto-submitting on correct digit count checks it. Run a 60-second countdown timer that starts on the first answer. Award points per correct answer scaled by difficulty, build a streak multiplier for consecutive correct answers, and flash green for right / red for wrong with a quick shake. Increase difficulty (larger operands, more multiplication/division) as the score climbs. Show live score, time remaining, current streak, and questions answered. At the end show a results screen with final score, accuracy, best streak, and problems-per-minute, plus a 'Play Again' button. Include difficulty selection (Easy/Medium/Hard) before starting and keep the input auto-focused.

title: Whack-a-Mole
brief: Timed whack-a-mole on a grid with pop animations, scoring, and a bomb
prompt: Build a Whack-a-Mole game as a single self-contained HTML file. Render a 3x3 (or 4x4) grid of holes. On a recurring interval, make a mole pop up from a random empty hole with a pop-up animation and stay visible for a short, gradually shortening window before retreating. Clicking/tapping a visible mole scores points, triggers a whack animation, and makes it disappear; missing or clicking an empty hole does not score. Occasionally spawn a 'bomb' mole that costs points or ends the game if hit, to add risk. Run a 30-second countdown; the spawn rate and mole speed increase as time passes for rising difficulty. Display live score, time remaining, and a hit/miss count. At game over, show final score with a 'Play Again' button. Use canvas or styled DOM elements with procedural mole art (no external images), satisfying animations, and sound via WebAudio synth for hits.

title: Fireworks Show
brief: Click-launched rockets that arc up and burst into gravity-pulled sparks
prompt: Build a fireworks display as a single self-contained HTML file. A full-window dark canvas where clicking anywhere launches a rocket from the bottom that arcs upward (with a glowing trail) toward the click point, then bursts into 60-120 colored spark particles that fly out radially, fade out, and fall under gravity with air drag. Add auto-launch so rockets fire on their own every 1-2 seconds even without clicks. Each burst picks a random vivid hue; some bursts should use a multi-color or ring pattern. Sparks must leave fading streak trails (use a translucent fill-rect overlay each frame rather than full clear) and shrink/dim before disappearing. Include subtle gravity, a faint twinkle flicker on sparks, and a small launch flash. Maintain a smooth requestAnimationFrame loop and recycle dead particles. No external assets — all canvas 2D and JS inline.

title: Rainstorm + Splashes
brief: Falling rain streaks that splash into ripple droplets on hitting the ground
prompt: Create a rainstorm animation as a single self-contained HTML file. A full-window canvas shows many falling rain drops drawn as thin vertical/slightly-angled streaks at varying speeds and depths (closer drops are longer, faster, brighter; far ones thin and faint for a parallax feel). The rain falls at a slight wind angle. When a drop reaches a ground line near the bottom, it spawns a small splash: 3-6 tiny droplet particles that pop upward and arc back down under gravity, plus an expanding fading ring ripple on the ground. Continuously emit new drops from the top so density stays constant. Add a slider or auto-varying wind that shifts the rain angle, and gentle distant lightning flashes that briefly brighten the whole scene every several seconds. Dark stormy background. Smooth rAF loop, particle recycling, all inline, no external assets.

title: Cursor Attractor Field
brief: Thousands of particles orbiting and swarming toward the mouse with trails
prompt: Build an interactive particle attractor as a single self-contained HTML file. A full-window canvas holds 1500-3000 small particles that are continuously pulled toward the mouse cursor by an inverse-distance force, giving them orbital, swirling motion rather than collapsing to a point (apply velocity damping and a max speed). Particles leave motion trails via a translucent overlay each frame. Color each particle by its speed or by distance to the cursor using an HSL gradient, creating flowing rivers of color. On mouse-down, flip the force to REPEL so particles scatter explosively, then return to attraction on release. Add a subtle ambient drift so the field stays alive when the mouse is idle. Keep it performant at the high particle count (typed arrays or tight loops encouraged). All canvas 2D + JS inline, no external assets.

title: Chaotic Double Pendulum
brief: Canvas double pendulum with accurate Lagrangian physics and motion trail
prompt: Build a chaotic double pendulum simulation as a single self-contained HTML file. Render two rigid arms on a canvas, anchored at a fixed pivot, with two bob masses. Integrate the true double-pendulum equations of motion (Lagrangian / coupled angular accelerations using both masses, both lengths, and gravity) via a small fixed timestep (e.g. RK4 or semi-implicit Euler) so the motion is visibly chaotic. Draw a fading trail tracing the path of the lower bob using a gradient/alpha decay. Let the user click-and-drag either bob to reposition it and release to drop (velocities reset to zero on grab). Provide sliders for gravity, the two arm lengths, the two masses, and a damping factor, plus Pause and Reset buttons. Show the current energy or angles as a small readout. Use smooth requestAnimationFrame animation, a dark background, and clean anti-aliased line rendering. All CSS and JS inline; no external resources.

title: Spring Cloth Mesh
brief: Verlet cloth grid pinned at top, draggable and tearable under gravity
prompt: Build an interactive cloth simulation as a single self-contained HTML file. Create a grid of point masses (e.g. 25x20) connected by structural spring constraints to their neighbors, simulated with Verlet integration and multiple constraint-relaxation iterations per frame for stiffness. Pin the top row of points so the cloth hangs and sways under gravity. Render the cloth on a canvas as a wireframe mesh or filled quads that shade based on stretch/strain. Let the user click-and-drag any point to pull the fabric, and support tearing: constraints that stretch beyond a threshold break. Add wind that gently pushes the cloth (toggle or slider), plus sliders for gravity and stiffness, and a Reset button. Animate smoothly with requestAnimationFrame. All CSS and JS inline; no external resources, images, or libraries.

title: N-Body Orbital Gravity
brief: Newtonian n-body sim with orbiting bodies, trails, and click-to-add mass
prompt: Build an N-body gravitational simulation as a single self-contained HTML file. Simulate several point masses on a canvas attracting each other via Newtonian gravity (F = G*m1*m2 / r^2) with softening to avoid singularities, integrated with a stable small-timestep scheme (leapfrog/velocity-Verlet preferred). Start with a sensible preset such as a central star plus a few stably orbiting planets. Draw each body sized by mass with a fading orbital trail behind it. Let the user click-drag on empty space to place a new body, dragging to set its initial velocity vector (show a drag arrow), and release to launch it into the simulation. Provide controls for gravity strength and simulation speed, a toggle for trails, and Pause/Reset buttons. Color bodies distinctly and render on a starfield-dark background. Use requestAnimationFrame. All CSS and JS inline; no external resources.

title: Newton's Cradle
brief: Pendulum-physics Newton's cradle with momentum transfer and metallic balls
prompt: Build a Newton's cradle simulation as a single self-contained HTML file. Render five identical balls hanging from a frame on equal-length strings, drawn with a metallic radial-gradient sheen and string lines on a canvas or SVG. Simulate each ball as a pendulum (gravity-driven angular motion) and detect collisions between adjacent touching balls, transferring momentum via elastic collision so that lifting and releasing one end ball launches exactly one ball off the other end (and two balls launch two, etc.). Let the user click-and-drag any end ball outward and release to start the motion; clicking should pause/grab cleanly. Include a slight energy damping so motion slowly decays realistically. Provide a slider for the number of balls and for damping, plus a Reset button. Keep the swing geometry accurate (balls stay on their string arcs). Animate with requestAnimationFrame. All CSS and JS inline; no external resources.

title: Water Ripple Tank
brief: 2D wave-equation ripple tank with click-to-drop and interference patterns
prompt: Build a water ripple tank simulation as a single self-contained HTML file. Implement a 2D wave-propagation grid (finite-difference wave equation or the classic height/velocity damping algorithm) rendered to a canvas via direct pixel manipulation (ImageData). Clicking anywhere drops a disturbance that radiates outward as expanding ring waves; multiple drops produce realistic interference patterns with visible constructive/destructive fringes. Shade the surface using brightness based on wave height plus a simple fake-lighting/refraction effect so ripples look like water. Add an optional pair of continuously oscillating point sources (toggle) to demonstrate a stable two-source interference pattern. Include sliders for wave damping and propagation speed, and a Clear button. Keep it performant on a reasonably sized grid and animate with requestAnimationFrame. All CSS and JS inline; no external resources or images.

title: Perlin Flow Field
brief: Thousands of particles streaming through an animated Perlin-noise flow field.
prompt: Build an animated Perlin-noise flow field as a single self-contained HTML file. Implement your own value or Perlin/simplex noise (no libraries). Render to a full-window canvas. Spawn at least 1500 particles that advect through a vector field whose angles come from sampling the noise function at each particle's position (angle = noise(x*scale, y*scale, time)*TAU*2). Each particle moves along the field, leaves a faint fading trail (draw a semi-transparent black rectangle over the whole canvas each frame instead of clearing), and wraps or respawns when it exits the screen. Color particles by their velocity direction or position using an evolving HSL palette so the whole field slowly shifts hue over time. Slowly animate the noise z-offset so the field morphs continuously. Run at a smooth animation-frame loop and handle window resize. Add subtle controls (keyboard or on-screen sliders) for particle count and noise scale. The result must be mesmerizing, performant, and fully offline.

title: Growing L-System Tree
brief: Recursive L-system plant that animates its growth branch by branch.
prompt: Create an animated L-system fractal plant as a single self-contained HTML file. Implement a generic L-system: define an axiom and production rules, expand them for a configurable number of iterations, then interpret the resulting string with a turtle (F = draw forward, +/- = turn, [ = push state, ] = pop state). Render on a canvas. The key feature is ANIMATED GROWTH: progressively reveal the structure over time (e.g. interpolate segment lengths from 0 to full, or draw the string incrementally) so the plant appears to grow from the ground up, with branches sprouting recursively. Add slight randomized angle jitter per branch for an organic look, taper line width with depth (thick trunk, thin twigs), and tint branches from brown at the base to green/pink blossoms at the tips. Include a few selectable presets (e.g. classic plant, bushy fern, fractal tree) and a button to regrow with a new random seed. Use a gentle sway animation once grown. Must run fully offline with no external assets.

title: Animated Julia Set
brief: Smooth-colored Julia set whose parameter c orbits, with mouse zoom.
prompt: Build an animated Julia set explorer as a single self-contained HTML file. Compute the Julia set on a canvas by iterating z = z^2 + c per pixel with an escape-time test (at least 100 max iterations). Animate the complex parameter c so it traces a smooth path over time (e.g. c = 0.7885 * e^(i*theta) with theta advancing each frame), making the fractal continuously morph through different shapes. Use SMOOTH (continuous) coloring via the normalized iteration count and a cyclic color palette so banding is minimized and colors are vivid. Let the user pan by dragging and zoom with the mouse wheel toward the cursor, recomputing at the new view. For performance, render at a reduced internal resolution while interacting and refine when idle, and consider chunked/row-based rendering to keep the UI responsive. Show the current c value and zoom level as an overlay. Everything inline, no external resources.

title: Voronoi Shatter
brief: Animated Voronoi diagram with drifting sites that shatters and reforms.
prompt: Create an animated Voronoi 'shatter' artwork as a single self-contained HTML file. Place 30-60 moving seed points (sites) that drift slowly around a full-window canvas, bouncing off edges. Each frame, compute the Voronoi diagram and fill every cell with a distinct color (smoothly interpolated from a palette, optionally weighted by cell area or distance to nearest neighbor). You may compute Voronoi via your own approach (e.g. per-pixel nearest-site on a downscaled grid, or a Fortune/Delaunay implementation) — no external libraries. Draw crisp cell borders so the plane looks like cracked glass or stained glass. Add a 'shatter' interaction: clicking explodes the cells outward (each cell's site flies apart with velocity, then eases back), and the diagram visibly fractures and reforms. Animate cell colors slowly cycling. Keep it smooth and handle resize. Fully self-contained and offline.

title: Maze Generator + Solver
brief: Watch a maze carve itself, then get solved by an animated pathfinder.
prompt: Build an animated maze generator and solver as a single self-contained HTML file. On a grid (e.g. 30x30 cells) drawn on a canvas, animate the GENERATION step by step using a randomized depth-first search (recursive backtracker): show the current cell highlighted, walls being knocked down, and visited cells tinted as the carving frontier advances — not just the finished maze. Once generation completes, automatically animate SOLVING from the top-left to the bottom-right using BFS or A*, visualizing the search frontier expanding (color cells by visit order, like a heat wave) and then tracing the final shortest path in a bright highlighted color. Provide buttons to regenerate, change grid size, and adjust animation speed. Use clean styling, smooth per-frame stepping, and ensure the maze is always perfect (exactly one path between any two cells). Everything inline, no external assets or libraries.

title: Game of Life Cosmos
brief: Conway's Life on a canvas grid with patterns, controls, and live stats
prompt: Build Conway's Game of Life as a single self-contained HTML file. Render a grid (at least 80x60 cells) on a <canvas>. Implement the standard B3/S23 rules with a toroidal (wrapping) world updated on a steady tick. Include a control bar with: Play/Pause, Step (advance one generation), Clear, and Randomize (seed ~25% live cells). Add a speed slider (e.g. 2 to 30 generations/sec) and a pattern picker that stamps at least 4 named seeds onto the grid: a Glider, a Lightweight Spaceship, a Gosper Glider Gun, and a Pulsar. Let the user click or click-drag on the grid to toggle cells while paused. Show a live readout of the current generation count and live-cell population. Use color so cells fade or tint by age (e.g. newly born vs long-lived cells differ), on a dark background. Everything must run offline with no external resources.

title: Slime Mold Trails
brief: Physarum agents depositing & sensing a pheromone field into emergent networks
prompt: Create a slime-mold (Physarum) trail simulation as a single self-contained HTML file. Spawn at least 3000 agents on a full-window <canvas>, each with a position and heading. Each frame every agent: senses a pheromone/trail field at three points (front-left, front, front-right) within a configurable sensor angle and distance, rotates toward the strongest sensed value, moves forward a fixed step, and deposits trail at its new location. Maintain the trail field as a buffer that diffuses (blurs slightly) and evaporates (decays) every frame, producing emergent vein-like networks. Render the field with a vivid color ramp on a dark background. Provide sliders for sensor angle, sensor distance, turn speed, and evaporation rate, plus a Reset button that re-scatters agents. Wrap agents at the edges. Target smooth animation via requestAnimationFrame. No external assets, CDNs, or network calls.

title: Reaction-Diffusion
brief: Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion growing organic Turing patterns with presets
prompt: Build a Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion simulator as a single self-contained HTML file. On a grid of at least 200x200 cells, simulate two chemicals U and V using the Gray-Scott equations with diffusion (use a Laplacian/9-point stencil), feed rate, and kill rate, running several solver steps per rendered frame for speed. Initialize U=1 everywhere with a few seeded patches of V, and render V to a <canvas> with a smooth color gradient. Provide sliders for feed rate, kill rate, and diffusion ratio, plus a preset dropdown with at least 4 named pattern regimes (e.g. Coral, Mitosis, Spots/Worms, Maze) that set the parameters to known interesting values. Let the user click/drag on the canvas to inject V (paint seeds). Include a Reset button. Keep it performant (e.g. typed arrays). It must run fully offline with no external resources.

title: Langton's Ant Colony
brief: Multiple Turing-machine ants on a multi-color grid building emergent highways
prompt: Create a Langton's Ant simulation as a single self-contained HTML file. Render a grid on a <canvas> (at least 120x90 cells). Implement one or more ants that each step: read the current cell's state, turn (right on one color, left on another), flip the cell to the next state, then move forward one cell, wrapping at edges. Support the classic 2-color rule (RL) AND a configurable turn-rule string of arbitrary length (e.g. 'RLR' or 'LLRR') that maps each color to a left/right turn and cycles through that many cell colors, producing different emergent structures. Provide a control bar with Play/Pause, Step, Reset, a steps-per-frame speed slider, a text input for the turn rule, and a control to set the number of ants (1 to 8) placed at random positions/headings. Color cells by their state and draw the ant(s) distinctly. Show the current step count. After many thousands of steps the classic rule should visibly build a 'highway'. No external assets or network calls; runs fully offline.

title: Classic Plasma Effect
brief: Animated sine-based plasma field with shifting palette on a full-window canvas
prompt: Build a classic demoscene plasma effect as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources, fully offline). Fill the full browser window with a canvas. Each frame, compute a per-pixel plasma value by summing several sine functions of x, y, distance from moving centers, and time, then map that value through a smoothly cycling color palette so the whole field continuously morphs and the colors rotate. Render by writing directly to an ImageData buffer (use a reduced internal resolution upscaled to fill the window so it stays smooth at 30+ fps). Animate continuously via requestAnimationFrame with a time variable driving the motion. Add a subtle FPS counter in a corner, and a key or click to toggle between at least 2 distinct palettes (e.g. fire vs. ocean). Resize the canvas correctly when the window resizes. The result should look like a flowing, organic, colorful field with no banding artifacts and no flicker.

title: Rotating Wireframe Torus
brief: 3D wireframe torus rotating on all axes with perspective projection on canvas
prompt: Create a rotating 3D wireframe torus (donut) as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources, fully offline). On a centered canvas with a dark background, generate the torus as a mesh of vertices (e.g. 24 segments around the tube by 48 around the ring) connected by edges. Each frame, rotate the vertices around the X, Y, and Z axes at different speeds using 3D rotation matrices, apply perspective projection (divide by depth) to map to 2D screen coordinates, and draw the connecting edges as lines. Shade or color edges by depth (farther edges dimmer / cooler) so the 3D form reads clearly, and optionally backface-cull or simply draw all edges. Animate smoothly via requestAnimationFrame. Provide mouse-drag to adjust rotation speed or orientation, and keep it centered and responsive when the window resizes. The torus should appear as a clean, recognizably 3D rotating wireframe with proper perspective foreshortening.

title: Texture Tunnel Effect
brief: Infinite pseudo-3D tunnel with scrolling procedural texture and twist
prompt: Build a classic pseudo-3D tunnel effect as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources, fully offline). Fill the window with a canvas rendered via a per-pixel ImageData buffer at a reduced internal resolution. For each pixel, compute its angle and distance from the screen center, then map (angle, 1/distance) into texture coordinates of a procedurally generated tile pattern (e.g. a checkerboard or XOR pattern) to create the illusion of flying through an infinite circular tunnel. Animate by scrolling the texture along the depth (distance) axis over time and slowly rotating the angle component so the tunnel appears to twist. Darken pixels near the center (far away) for a depth-fog vignette. Keep it running at 30+ fps via requestAnimationFrame, and handle window resize. The effect should give a convincing sense of continuous forward motion into a glowing, twisting tunnel.

title: Matrix Digital Rain
brief: Cascading green glyph columns with fading trails and head glow on canvas
prompt: Create the Matrix digital rain effect as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources, fully offline). On a full-window black canvas, render many vertical columns of falling characters. Each column has its own speed and a stream of glyphs drawn from a fixed character set (use katakana-like or ASCII symbols generated procedurally — no external fonts). The leading character of each stream is bright (near-white/bright green) with a glow, and characters trailing behind it fade progressively to dark green, creating a comet-like trail. Achieve the trail by drawing a translucent black rectangle over the whole canvas each frame instead of fully clearing it. Randomly mutate some characters in place as they fall, and recycle columns to new random start positions when they run off the bottom. Run via requestAnimationFrame, recompute the column grid on window resize, and keep it smooth at 30+ fps. It should look like the iconic cascading green code rain.

title: Glowing Metaballs
brief: Smooth blobby metaballs merging via scalar field with threshold color bands
prompt: Build an animated metaballs effect as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources, fully offline). On a full-window canvas rendered through an ImageData buffer at a reduced internal resolution, simulate 5 to 8 moving 'balls', each with a position and radius, drifting around the screen and bouncing off the edges. For every pixel, compute the summed scalar field value = sum over balls of radius^2 / distance^2, then color the pixel based on that field: pixels above a threshold form smooth merging blobs, and use 2 or 3 nested thresholds to draw glowing color bands (contour-like) so blobs have a soft halo and a bright core. The blobs must visibly merge and separate organically as they move. Animate via requestAnimationFrame at 30+ fps, handle window resize, and allow a click to spawn an extra ball at the cursor. The result should be smooth, gooey, glowing metaballs with no hard pixel edges.

title: Sorting Algorithm Visualizer
brief: Animated bar visualizer comparing bubble, quick, merge & heap sort with controls
prompt: Build a sorting-algorithm visualizer as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources, runs fully offline). Render an array of 40-60 vertical bars whose heights represent values. Provide a dropdown to choose among at least four algorithms: Bubble Sort, Quick Sort, Merge Sort, and Heap Sort. Provide buttons to Shuffle (randomize the array), Start/Pause, and Step, plus a speed slider. During sorting, animate each comparison and swap by color-coding bars: highlight the two bars being compared in one color, mark swapped/written bars in another, and color bars green once they are in their final sorted position. Show a live counter of comparisons and array accesses/swaps, and display the algorithm's name and Big-O complexity. The animation must be smooth via requestAnimationFrame or timed steps, must correctly sort to ascending order every time, and must let the user switch algorithms and reshuffle without reloading. Use a clean dark UI.

title: A* Pathfinding Grid
brief: Interactive grid visualizing A* search expanding nodes to find the path
prompt: Create an A* pathfinding visualizer on a grid as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external assets, fully offline). Draw a grid of at least 25x25 cells on a canvas or with DOM cells. Let the user click-and-drag to paint walls/obstacles, and drag the start (green) and goal (red) cells to reposition them. Provide buttons to Run, Pause/Step, Clear walls, and Reset, plus a maze-randomize button. When run, animate the A* algorithm step by step: shade cells in the open set and closed set in distinct colors as they are evaluated, and once the goal is reached animate the reconstructed shortest path as a highlighted line of cells. Display live stats: nodes visited and final path length. Support diagonal-vs-orthogonal movement via a toggle and show the correct shortest path for the chosen movement. Handle the no-path-possible case gracefully with a message. Use crisp colors and a responsive layout.

title: Skinnable Analog Clock
brief: Smooth real-time analog clock with sweeping hands, ticks and digital readout
prompt: Build an animated analog clock as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources, fully offline). Render the clock face on a canvas or with SVG: a circular dial with 12 hour numerals, 60 minute tick marks (hour ticks longer/bolder), and three hands (hour, minute, second) of distinct lengths and widths with a center hub. Drive it from the real system time and update via requestAnimationFrame so the second hand sweeps smoothly (sub-second interpolation), not in discrete jumps. Hands must be positioned with correct angles including the hour hand advancing fractionally as minutes pass. Include a small digital HH:MM:SS readout below the dial and the current date. Add a toggle to switch the second hand between smooth-sweep and tick-once-per-second modes, and a toggle for a light/dark theme. Make the dial visually polished with subtle shadows or gradients drawn procedurally. It must keep accurate time indefinitely.

title: Fourier Epicycle Drawer
brief: Rotating epicycles summing sine waves to trace a periodic shape over time
prompt: Create a Fourier-series epicycle animation as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources, fully offline). On a canvas, draw a chain of rotating circles (epicycles): each circle rotates at an integer multiple of a base frequency, with the next circle attached to the rim of the previous one, and the tip of the final circle traces out a periodic waveform. Use this to approximate a target shape such as a square wave or a sawtooth via its Fourier series, accumulating the traced path over time into a persistent curve on the right side of the canvas, with a connecting line linking the rotating epicycles to the live plotted point. Provide a slider to control the number of harmonics/terms (e.g. 1 to 50) so the user sees the approximation sharpen as terms increase, a speed slider, and a dropdown to switch the target waveform (square, sawtooth, triangle). Animate smoothly with requestAnimationFrame, fade or recycle the traced curve each period, and use clean contrasting colors on a dark background.

title: Scientific Calculator
brief: Full scientific calculator with keypad, trig, logs, memory, and history
prompt: Build a scientific calculator as a single self-contained HTML file. Render a styled keypad with digits, decimal point, and operators (+, -, *, /). Include scientific functions: sin, cos, tan (in a toggleable DEG/RAD mode), ln, log10, square root, x^y power, x^2, factorial, 1/x, and constants pi and e. Support parentheses for grouping and respect correct operator precedence (multiplication/division before addition/subtraction). Show a two-line display: the current expression on top and the live or final result below. Include memory keys (MC, MR, M+, M-), a backspace key, a clear-entry (CE) and all-clear (AC), and a sign toggle. Maintain a scrollable history list of past calculations that the user can click to recall. Handle errors gracefully (e.g. divide by zero, factorial of negatives) by showing 'Error' rather than crashing. Support keyboard input for digits and operators. Style it cleanly with clear button hover/active states and a responsive grid layout.

title: Loan Amortization Calc
brief: Mortgage/loan calculator with monthly payment + full amortization schedule
prompt: Build a loan amortization calculator as a single self-contained HTML file. Provide inputs for loan amount (principal), annual interest rate (%), and term in years (with a months option). Compute the fixed monthly payment using the standard amortization formula and display it prominently along with total interest paid and total cost over the life of the loan. Generate a full month-by-month amortization schedule table showing payment number, payment amount, principal portion, interest portion, and remaining balance for each period; make this table scrollable. Render a simple inline SVG or canvas bar/line chart visualizing how the principal vs interest split shifts over time. Add an optional extra-monthly-payment field that, when used, recomputes the schedule and shows interest saved and months shortened. Recalculate live as inputs change, validate inputs (reject negatives/zeros gracefully), and format all currency values with thousands separators and two decimals. Style it as a clean financial dashboard.

title: Units Converter
brief: Multi-category unit converter: length, mass, temp, volume, speed, area
prompt: Build a multi-category units converter as a single self-contained HTML file. Offer category tabs or a dropdown for at least six categories: Length, Mass/Weight, Temperature, Volume, Speed, and Area. Each category exposes its relevant units (e.g. Length: mm, cm, m, km, inch, foot, yard, mile; Mass: mg, g, kg, tonne, ounce, pound, stone; Temperature: Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin; etc.). Provide a 'from' value input with a from-unit selector and a to-unit selector, and convert live as the user types or changes either unit. Handle temperature with the correct non-linear formulas (not simple ratios). Include a swap button to exchange the from/to units. Show the result with sensible precision and a clear 'X unit = Y unit' equation line. Optionally display a small reference table converting the entered value into all units in the current category at once. Validate non-numeric input gracefully. Style it with a clean, modern layout and smooth category switching.

title: Age & Date Calculator
brief: Age/date-difference calculator: years/months/days, next birthday, stats
prompt: Build an age and date-difference calculator as a single self-contained HTML file. Provide two date inputs: a 'from' date (e.g. date of birth) defaulting to a sensible value and a 'to' date defaulting to today. Compute the exact difference broken down into years, months, and days (using correct calendar math that accounts for varying month lengths and leap years), and also show the total difference expressed independently in months, weeks, days, hours, minutes, and seconds. Display the day of the week the 'from' date fell on. For a birthday use-case, show days remaining until the next birthday and which weekday it lands on. Include a live-updating seconds counter for the total-seconds figure. Validate that the 'to' date is not before the 'from' date (or handle the reversed case gracefully). Add quick-preset buttons (e.g. set 'to' to today). Style it as a clean card-based layout with each stat in its own tile.

title: Pomodoro Focus Timer
brief: Work/break Pomodoro timer with cycle tracking, ring progress, and controls
prompt: Build a Pomodoro focus timer as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources). Requirements: a large circular SVG/canvas progress ring that depletes as time counts down, plus a big MM:SS digital readout in the center. Three modes — Focus (25 min), Short Break (5 min), Long Break (15 min) — selectable via tabs, each with its own accent color. Start, Pause, and Reset buttons. Automatically cycle: after each completed Focus session increment a visible 'completed sessions' counter and auto-switch to a Short Break, and after every 4th Focus session switch to a Long Break instead, then auto-start the next Focus block. Show a row of 4 dots indicating progress through the current long-break cycle. Let the user edit the three durations via number inputs in a settings panel. On each session completion play a short WebAudio beep (synth only, no audio files) and flash the screen briefly. Keep all state in memory. Make it clean, centered, and responsive with smooth ring animation.

title: Kanban Board
brief: Drag-and-drop Kanban with 3 columns, add/edit/delete cards, live counts
prompt: Build a Kanban board as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources). Three columns: To Do, In Progress, Done. Each card shows a title, an optional description, and a colored priority tag (Low/Medium/High). Implement full HTML5 drag-and-drop: cards can be dragged between columns and reordered within a column, with a visible drop-placeholder/insertion indicator and a highlighted target column during drag. An 'Add card' button at the bottom of each column opens an inline form (title, description, priority). Cards can be edited (click to open editor) and deleted (x button). Each column header shows a live count of its cards. Include a global text filter that dims cards not matching the query. Use a polished card-shadow UI with smooth transitions. Keep all data in memory (no localStorage). Make columns scroll independently when full and stay responsive.

title: Habit Tracker Grid
brief: Multi-habit weekly grid with toggles, streaks, and completion stats
prompt: Build a habit tracker as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources). Show a grid where each row is a habit and each column is one of the last 30 days (most recent on the right), with day-of-month and weekday labels along the top. Clicking any cell toggles that habit as done/not-done for that day, filling it with the habit's chosen color (GitHub-contribution style). Let the user add a new habit (name + color picker) and delete habits. For each habit row display: the current streak (consecutive done days ending today), the longest streak, and a completion percentage over the visible window. Include an overall summary bar showing today's completion across all habits as a progress bar. Weekend columns should be subtly shaded. Keep all state in memory. Make it clean, scrollable horizontally if needed, and visually satisfying with hover highlights and smooth fill transitions.

title: Event Countdown
brief: Live countdown to a chosen date/time with flip-style D/H/M/S units
prompt: Build an event countdown timer as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources). The user enters an event name and picks a target date and time via inputs. Display a large live-updating countdown broken into Days, Hours, Minutes, Seconds, each in its own card with a flip/odometer-style animation when the digit changes (CSS-driven, no images). Update every second. Below the countdown show a horizontal progress bar representing elapsed time from when the countdown was created to the target. Let the user save up to several countdowns and switch between them via a list/tabs, each showing a mini live preview of its remaining time. When a target is reached, show a celebratory state (animated confetti drawn on canvas, synth chime via WebAudio) and the message 'It's time!'. Handle past dates gracefully (show 'Event passed'). Keep all state in memory and make it responsive and elegant.

title: Weekly Schedule Planner
brief: 7-day x hourly grid; click-drag to create, move, resize colored events
prompt: Build a weekly schedule planner as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources). Render a calendar grid with 7 day columns (Mon-Sun) and hourly rows from 6:00 to 22:00, with time labels down the left and a faint current-time indicator line if today is in view. Click-and-drag vertically within a day column to create a new event spanning the dragged time range; on release prompt for a title and let the user pick a color category. Events render as colored blocks positioned and sized to their time span, showing title and start-end time. Events can be dragged to move them (across days and times) and resized by dragging their bottom edge, snapping to 15-minute increments. Clicking an event opens an editor to rename, recolor, or delete it. Show a small total-scheduled-hours summary. Prevent zero-length events. Keep all state in memory. Make it clean, with clear grid lines, hover affordances, and smooth dragging.

title: CSS Gradient Studio
brief: Multi-stop gradient builder with live preview and copyable CSS output
prompt: Build a CSS gradient generator as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS + JS inline, no external resources, fully offline). Provide a large live preview pane filling most of the screen. Support both LINEAR and RADIAL gradient types via a toggle, with an angle control (0-360 degree dial or slider) for linear gradients. Implement a draggable color-stop track below/under the preview: clicking the track adds a new color stop at that position, each stop shows a color swatch that opens a native color input on click, and stops can be dragged horizontally to reposition (0-100%) and deleted (require at least 2 stops). Show the live percentage of each stop. Provide a 'Random' button that generates a pleasing random gradient (random stop count 2-4, harmonious colors). Output the exact CSS `background` declaration in a read-only code box that updates live, with a 'Copy' button that copies it to the clipboard and shows a brief confirmation. Include a few preset gradient swatches the user can click to load. Use a clean modern UI with smooth interactions.

title: Password Generator
brief: Configurable password + passphrase generator with strength meter
prompt: Build a password generator as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS + JS inline, no external resources, fully offline). Offer two modes via tabs: RANDOM PASSWORD and PASSPHRASE. For random password: a length slider (4-64) with live numeric readout, and toggles for uppercase, lowercase, digits, and symbols, plus an 'exclude ambiguous characters' (O/0/l/1/I) option; at least one enabled category must be honored. For passphrase: a word-count slider (3-8), a separator selector (-, ., space, _), and toggles to capitalize words and append a random number; build phrases from a built-in embedded word list (include at least ~80 common words inline). Generate using window.crypto.getRandomValues for real randomness. Show the result in a large read-only field with a 'Copy' button (with confirmation) and a 'Regenerate' button. Display a STRENGTH METER computed from estimated entropy (bits), with a colored bar and a label (Weak/Fair/Strong/Excellent) plus the entropy value and an approximate crack-time estimate. Update everything live as options change. Use a polished modern UI.

title: Dice Roller
brief: RPG dice notation roller with animated dice and roll history
prompt: Build a tabletop dice roller as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS + JS inline, no external resources, fully offline). Provide quick-add buttons for standard dice (d4, d6, d8, d10, d12, d20, d100) that build up a pending pool, and ALSO accept standard dice-notation input like '2d6+3' or '1d20-1' or '4d8' parsed correctly (count, sides, and +/- modifier). On Roll, animate the dice with a brief tumbling/rolling effect (CSS or canvas), then display each individual die face value, the modifier, and the bold grand TOTAL. Render d6 faces with pip layouts and other dice as clearly numbered shapes. Maintain a scrollable ROLL HISTORY (most recent first) showing the notation, individual results, and total for each roll, with a clear-history button. Include an 'advantage/disadvantage' helper for d20 (roll twice, keep higher/lower, highlight which). Show running stats (number of rolls this session). Use a tactile, game-like UI with satisfying feedback.

title: Identicon Generator
brief: Deterministic symmetric avatar generator from any text seed
prompt: Build a deterministic identicon (avatar) generator as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS + JS inline, no external resources, fully offline). Provide a text input; as the user types a seed (name, email, anything), generate a unique symmetric pixel-grid avatar rendered on a canvas, derived purely from a hash of the input (implement a simple deterministic string hash inline — same input must always yield the same identicon). Use a grid (e.g. 5x5 or 7x7 cells) that is MIRRORED horizontally for pleasing symmetry, with foreground cells colored from a hue derived from the hash and a light background. Show a live GALLERY: render the same seed in multiple style variants simultaneously (e.g. classic GitHub-style blocks, a circular/dot variant, and a symmetric triangle/geometric variant) so the user sees several avatars at once. Include controls for grid density and avatar size, a 'Random seed' button, and a 'Download PNG' button that exports the selected avatar via canvas toDataURL (no network). Ensure identicons are visually distinct across different seeds and stable across re-renders. Use a clean UI with a subtle grid of example seeds.

title: JSON Tree Viewer
brief: Format/validate JSON with a collapsible, syntax-highlighted tree explorer.
prompt: Build a JSON formatter and collapsible tree viewer as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources, runs fully offline). Provide a large textarea on the left where the user pastes JSON and a tree panel on the right. On input (debounced), parse the JSON: if invalid, show a clear red error message with the line/column or character position of the failure; if valid, render a syntax-highlighted, expandable tree. Tree requirements: objects and arrays show as collapsible nodes with a triangle toggle that expands/collapses on click; show array indices and object keys, with a count badge like {3} or [5] of child entries; color-code by type (string, number, boolean, null distinct colors); strings shown quoted, null shown dimmed. Include 'Expand all' and 'Collapse all' buttons, a 'Format' (pretty-print with 2-space indent) button, a 'Minify' button, and a 'Copy' button for the current JSON. Provide a 'Load sample' button that injects a nested example object. Deep-nest indentation must use guide lines. Style it as a clean dark-mode dev tool with monospace font. Handle large/deeply nested input without crashing.

title: Regex Tester
brief: Live regex tester: highlighted matches, groups, flags, replace preview.
prompt: Build a live regular-expression tester as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources, runs fully offline). Layout: a regex input field with a separate flags input (g, i, m, s, u, y) shown as toggleable chips, and a large multi-line test-string textarea below. As the user types, evaluate the regex against the test string in real time and render the test string in a result panel with every match highlighted (alternating highlight colors for adjacent matches so they are visually distinct). If the regex is invalid, show the JS error message and do not crash. Show a match list/table: for each match show its index, the full matched text, the start offset, and any captured groups (numbered and, if present, named groups). Display a live count of total matches. Include a 'Replace' section with a replacement-pattern input (supporting $1, $&, named group refs) that shows the replaced-output preview live. Provide a small reference panel of common tokens (\d \w \s ^ $ etc.) and a 'Load example' button. Use a clean dark dev-tool aesthetic with monospace fonts. Guard against catastrophic input gracefully.

title: Color Picker & Palette
brief: Pick colors with HEX/RGB/HSL sync, sliders, and palette generator.
prompt: Build a color picker and palette tool as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources, runs fully offline). Center a large color preview swatch. Provide a 2D saturation/value picker area (a draggable gradient square) plus a vertical hue slider, all drawn on canvas or with CSS gradients, fully draggable with the mouse. Show synchronized, editable inputs for HEX (#RRGGBB), RGB (three 0-255 fields), and HSL (h 0-360, s/l 0-100%): editing any one updates the swatch and all the others live and correctly. Provide separate range sliders for R, G, B and for H, S, L. Add an alpha slider and show RGBA/HSLA output too. Include a 'Copy' button for each format. Generate a harmony palette section that, from the current color, shows complementary, triadic, and analogous swatches (click any to adopt it). Show a row of recently picked colors. Display the WCAG contrast ratio of the current color against white and against black with pass/fail (AA) labels. Style it as a polished modern tool. All conversions must be mathematically accurate.

title: Text Diff Viewer
brief: Line-by-line diff of two texts with added/removed highlighting + stats.
prompt: Build a two-pane text diff viewer as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources, runs fully offline). Provide two editable textareas (Original and Changed) at the top with a 'Compare' action (also recompute live on edit, debounced). Implement an actual line-level diff using a longest-common-subsequence algorithm (do not just compare line N to line N) so that inserted or deleted lines shift correctly. Render the result as a side-by-side view with aligned rows: unchanged lines plain, removed lines highlighted red on the left, added lines highlighted green on the right, and gap placeholders where one side has no corresponding line. Show line numbers for each side. Add a toggle for an inline/unified view (single column with +/- prefixed colored lines). For modified lines, additionally highlight the changed words/characters within the line. Show a summary bar with counts of added, removed, and unchanged lines. Include options to ignore leading/trailing whitespace and to ignore case. Provide 'Swap sides' and 'Load sample' buttons. Use a clean dark dev-tool look with a monospace font.

title: Encoder Toolbox
brief: Convert text via Base64, URL, hex, binary, and case with live two-way sync.
prompt: Build a multi-format encoder/decoder toolbox as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources, runs fully offline). Provide an input textarea and an output textarea. Offer a set of conversion modes selectable via a tab or dropdown: Base64 encode, Base64 decode, URL/percent encode, URL decode, HTML-entity encode, HTML-entity decode, text-to-hex, hex-to-text, text-to-binary (8-bit, space separated), and binary-to-text. Conversion runs live as the user types or changes mode. Include a 'Swap' button that moves the output into the input (so chains of conversions are easy), a 'Copy output' button, and a 'Clear' button. Base64 must handle full Unicode correctly (UTF-8 aware, not raw btoa on multibyte). On a decode error (e.g. malformed Base64 or hex), show a clear inline error instead of crashing. Also show a small live stats line for the input: character count, byte count (UTF-8), and word count. Add a case-transform section (UPPER, lower, Title Case, camelCase, snake_case) that transforms the input. Style as a tidy dark dev tool with monospace I/O fields.

title: WebAudio Synth Keyboard
brief: Playable 2-octave synth keyboard with waveform, ADSR, filter & arpeggiator
prompt: Build a polyphonic WebAudio synthesizer keyboard as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources, no network calls, runs fully offline). Requirements: (1) Render a 2-octave piano keyboard (white + black keys correctly positioned) that is playable by both mouse/touch click and computer keys (map a-k row to white keys, w/e/t/y/u etc. to sharps); show the active key highlighted while held. (2) Generate all sound with WebAudio oscillators — support polyphony (multiple simultaneous notes) with correct equal-temperament frequencies. (3) Provide controls: waveform selector (sine/square/sawtooth/triangle), an ADSR envelope (attack, decay, sustain, release sliders) applied to each note via a GainNode, a low-pass filter with cutoff + resonance sliders, and a master volume. (4) Include an octave shift control and a simple arpeggiator toggle with adjustable rate that cycles held notes. (5) Show a small live oscilloscope or frequency readout driven by an AnalyserNode. Style it as a polished hardware synth panel with labeled knobs/sliders, clean layout, and responsive key feedback. Must initialize/resume AudioContext on first user gesture.

title: Pixel Art Editor
brief: Grid pixel editor: palette, draw/erase/fill, undo, mirror, PNG export
prompt: Build a pixel-art editor as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources, no network calls, runs fully offline). Requirements: (1) A configurable square pixel grid (default 16x16, with options for 8/16/32) drawn on a canvas, scaled up so each pixel is a large clickable cell with visible grid lines. (2) Tools: pencil (click or click-drag to paint), eraser, bucket fill (flood fill of contiguous same-color region), eyedropper (pick color from a cell), and a clear-all button. (3) A color palette of preset swatches plus a custom color picker; show the currently selected color. (4) Undo and redo (at least 20 steps of history). (5) A live mirror/symmetry mode toggle (horizontal) so painting mirrors across the vertical axis. (6) Adjustable grid-line visibility toggle and a checkerboard transparent background. (7) Export the artwork as a PNG download at an upscaled resolution (e.g. 16x via nearest-neighbor) using canvas.toDataURL and an anchor download — preserving crisp pixels (no smoothing). Style it as a clean, modern editor with a toolbar, palette panel, and the canvas centered. Painting must feel smooth and responsive with drag support.

title: Step Sequencer Drum Machine
brief: 16-step synth drum machine: kick/snare/hat/clap, BPM, swing, per-track volume
prompt: Build a step-sequencer drum machine as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources or audio files, no network calls, runs fully offline). Requirements: (1) A grid of at least 4 instrument tracks (kick, snare, closed hi-hat, clap) each with 16 toggleable step cells; clicking a cell toggles whether that drum fires on that step. (2) Synthesize ALL drum sounds procedurally with WebAudio — e.g. kick = pitch-swept sine with fast amp decay, snare/hat = filtered noise bursts (noise via a buffer of random samples), clap = layered noise envelopes. No samples. (3) A transport: play/stop, a BPM control (e.g. 60-200) with accurate scheduled timing using AudioContext.currentTime look-ahead scheduling (not setInterval-driven oscillator starts), and a moving playhead that highlights the current step in sync with audio. (4) Per-track mute and volume, plus a master volume. (5) A swing/shuffle amount slider that delays odd steps. (6) Clear-all and a randomize-pattern button. Style it as a polished drum-machine UI: labeled tracks on the left, step cells lighting up on each beat, accent on downbeats (every 4th step visually distinct). Resume AudioContext on first user gesture; timing must stay tight at high BPM.

title: Spirograph Drawing Studio
brief: Animated spirograph generator with gear ratios, color cycling, PNG export
prompt: Build a spirograph drawing studio as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources, no network calls, runs fully offline). Requirements: (1) Render mathematically generated hypotrochoid/epitrochoid curves on a canvas using controllable parameters: fixed ring radius R, rolling circle radius r, and pen offset d — exposed as sliders that update the curve. (2) An animate/draw mode that progressively traces the curve over time (the pen moves along the path so the user watches it being drawn), with adjustable drawing speed, plus an instant-render toggle. (3) Color controls: a solid color picker AND a rainbow/hue-cycling mode where the stroke hue shifts along the curve; adjustable line width and opacity for layered translucent passes. (4) Allow layering multiple spirographs (a button to lock the current curve and start a new one with different params on top of it), building up a composition. (5) Show the current numeric parameters and the number of petals/cusps. (6) A clear button and a background color choice (light/dark). (7) Export the canvas as a PNG download. Style it as an elegant creative tool with a control panel and a large centered canvas; the animated tracing should be smooth via requestAnimationFrame.

title: Cosmic Zoom
brief: Pinch and scroll across 62 orders of magnitude, from a proton to the observable universe, with a fact card at every scale.
prompt: Build a complete scale-of-the-universe explorer as a single self-contained HTML file (all CSS and JS inline, no external resources, runs fully offline). Embed an inline curated dataset of exactly 36 objects spanning 10^-15 m (proton) to 10^27 m (observable universe) — e.g. proton, hydrogen atom, DNA helix, virus, red blood cell, dust mite, grain of rice, honeybee, human, blue whale, redwood, Burj Khalifa, Mount Everest, neutron star, Moon, Earth, Jupiter, Sun, Betelgeuse, Solar System, Oort Cloud, Milky Way, Local Group — each entry holding name, size in meters, a one-sentence fact, and a distinct procedural canvas glyph (colored circle/ellipse/ring/silhouette, no images). Render a centered canvas stage where each object is visible only while its size is within ~2.5 orders of magnitude of the current zoom scale, drawn proportionally and cross-faded in/out as it enters/leaves range. Controls: mouse wheel and vertical drag zoom smoothly on a logarithmic scale, pinch-to-zoom on touch, Left/Right arrows snap to the previous/next object, and clicking/tapping any object frames it and opens its fact card. HUD: a persistent top bar showing the current scale as 10^N meters plus a human-readable comparison label, and a draggable log-scale minimap slider along the bottom with tick marks at every power of ten and dots marking all 36 objects. Use delta-time critically-damped easing for all zoom motion so it is framerate-independent and never jumps; play a short synthesized WebAudio tick each time an order-of-magnitude boundary is crossed and a soft filtered-noise whoosh whose gain follows zoom velocity, behind a mute toggle. Background hue drifts with scale (deep violet at quantum scales through blue-black space at cosmic scales). Include a Reset button that eases back to human scale (10^0 m).

title: Orbit Lab
